128131.explanation: For the SGP, qualitatively good administrators are more important than an imposed male/female ratio.

128132.explanation: SGP supports gathering opinions but opposes binding referenda. Residents elect a municipal council. Council members are representatives of the people who represent residents. Of course, the municipality can use resident panels to gauge opinions.

128136.explanation: SGP considers subsidies to stimulate initiatives negotiable but opposes structural support.

128137.explanation: To offer perspective to local entrepreneurs, the SGP sees that our land is urgently needed to allow our own entrepreneurs to expand.

128138.explanation: The SGP sees no obligation here. However, under the Distribution Act (national legislation), every municipality is required to shelter asylum seekers. The SGP believes this must fit within conditions set by the municipal council.

128143.explanation: SGP believes this does not belong to the tasks of a municipality.

128144.explanation: Nothing is free. In Zwartewaterland, the policy is 'the polluter pays,' and the SGP considers this a good system.

128147.explanation: SGP primarily views the rural area as a domain for farmers and acknowledges that agricultural land is valuable. However, SGP sees possibilities to realize homes on existing farmyards.

128149.explanation: The local broadcaster is already financially supported by the municipality. According to the SGP, an extra contribution is unnecessary.

128151.explanation: Music lessons and memberships in sports clubs can be paid for by parents themselves. Residents with low incomes can already apply for subsidies.

128152.explanation: SGP believes sports clubs are responsible for healthy financial management. Naturally, the municipality wants to assist when problems arise.

128154.explanation: Libraries serve a broader function than just lending books and therefore must be accessible nearby.

128155.explanation: There is much talk about enforcement, but the availability of municipal enforcement officers (boa's) is scarce.

128156.explanation: SGP believes camera surveillance should be used sparingly and only in exceptional cases.

128157.explanation: There are agreements on this at the regional level, which SGP considers to be functioning sufficiently.

128162.explanation: Infill locations are not always available, and the housing shortage is significant.

128163.explanation: When possible and feasible, SGP believes this should certainly be allowed. Contributing to society is valuable for everyone.

128167.explanation: SGP thinks this should be addressed based on demand with a good balance between electric cars and fuel-powered cars.

128169.explanation: SGP values identity-based care highly. There must be a balanced offer among different providers.

128172.explanation: In recent years, proceeds from this tax have been used to place dog waste bins, and dog play areas have been realized in all centers.

128175.explanation: SGP supports Sunday as a collective day of rest. Zwartewaterland has no shopping Sundays and wishes to keep it that way in the future.

128181.explanation: SGP is not in favor of apartment buildings. However, Zwartewaterland does have attractive examples of appropriate tall apartment buildings.

128182.explanation: SGP thinks this goes too far and will lead to confusing situations.

128187.explanation: SGP believes this should not be a goal in itself. There must be a careful consideration of what young people need. A youth center usually serves only a limited target group.

128131.explanation: Equal opportunities for men and women are important, but fixed quotas should not be decisive in appointments.

128132.explanation: We believe the municipal council is the designated body to make decisions after careful consideration and debate. We gladly involve residents through participation and consultation, but a referendum does not fit here.

128136.explanation: We support local initiatives for sustainable energy. When residents generate energy together, it contributes to involvement and sustainability.

128137.explanation: We want to manage space and landscape carefully in Zwartewaterland. Distribution centers often generate a lot of traffic and provide little local added value. Therefore, the municipality must be cautious and oppose new establishments.

128138.explanation: We stand for hospitality and responsibility but not for one large-scale asylum seekers center (AZC). ChristenUnie opts for small-scale, humane shelters spread over the centers.

128143.explanation: It is not a municipal task. In consultation and with a proper permit, an initiative supported by the residents is a possibility.

128144.explanation: We believe that the polluter should pay. Collecting bulky waste for free leads to more waste and higher costs for everyone. It is reasonable that residents take responsibility for offering and disposing of bulky waste themselves.

128147.explanation: We see opportunities for farmers to build homes on their own land. This can contribute to the vitality of the rural area, provided it is done carefully and fits the landscape and surroundings.

128149.explanation: A strong local broadcaster contributes to involvement, information provision, and connection in our municipality. Therefore, we believe the municipality should financially support the local broadcaster where necessary.

128151.explanation: Music education contributes to children's development. Every primary school child should have the chance to take music lessons, regardless of their parents' income. That is why we support municipal subsidies.

128152.explanation: It is not the municipality's task, but the responsibility of the associations themselves.

128154.explanation: Reading is an important value for development and equal opportunities. Libraries should therefore remain nearby and accessible. One central library increases the distance; we choose strong, local libraries.

128155.explanation: There are sufficient enforcement officers active in Zwartewaterland. Extra community enforcement officers are therefore not needed. We opt for efficient use of capacity and cooperation, rather than increasing the number of enforcers.

128156.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on our residents' privacy. Only in exceptional circumstances can this form of surveillance be considered.

128157.explanation: Good and safe shelter in cases of domestic violence is essential. This is already organized in our region. We consider it important that these regional facilities continue to function well, rather than arranging this separately per municipality.

128162.explanation: Housing is a social fundamental right. If necessary, thinking outside the box is required to reduce housing shortages.

128163.explanation: We believe that people on social assistance need support and perspective, not obligations that feel like coercion. We encourage participation gladly, but always based on trust, customization, and what a person can handle.

128167.explanation: Electric driving is becoming increasingly normal. By designating more parking spaces as charging points, we respond practically to this and support the transition to more sustainable transport, appropriate to the scale of Zwartewaterland.

128169.explanation: Care is about trust and proximity. People must be able to choose a care provider who fits their faith, culture, or belief. This contributes to appropriate care and respect for everyone's background.

128172.explanation: The dog tax contributes to financing facilities and maintenance in public spaces. Abolishing it means costs fall on all residents. We consider it reasonable that dog owners contribute to this.

128175.explanation: We value peace and space for families and retail employees. Especially for local entrepreneurs who work hard all week, a fixed day of rest is essential.

128179.explanation: This does not fit the character of our municipality and can have negative consequences for livability and safety.

128181.explanation: Only in places where there is support and it fits the landscape.

128182.explanation: It creates unnecessary pressure on livability in the streets and neighborhoods as parking. Student housing is not an issue in Zwartewaterland.

128187.explanation: Youth have the right to a safe place to meet each other. Therefore, a youth center must be possible in every village of Zwartewaterland.

128131.explanation: It is desirable that the composition is optimally made up of skilled people.

128136.explanation: Existing subsidy schemes are available for such projects.

128137.explanation: The municipality of Zwartewaterland must use potential industrial land sparingly, and furthermore, this has too great an impact on transportation movements.

128138.explanation: Zwartewaterland houses a large number of labor migrants and accommodates an above-average number of refugees. If capacity needs to be expanded, it should be proportionally distributed across the villages. However, this is challenging due to scarce land and building options because of Natura 2000 areas.

128143.explanation: When the individual fireworks ban is in effect, a fireworks show can be a nice and safe alternative.

128144.explanation: From a cost perspective, BGZ finds the current arrangements acceptable.

128147.explanation: Nationally, we have the Space for Space scheme. Where possible (limited) and regulated.

128149.explanation: Local and independent media must be able to continue to exist.

128151.explanation: BGZ is an advocate for cooperation between primary schools and a music provider.

128154.explanation: Each village has its own facility.

128155.explanation: According to national figures, the municipality of Zwartewaterland is a safe municipality.

128156.explanation: Camera surveillance only in extreme cases where it adds value. For example, in industrial areas.

128157.explanation: If there is a need for it, BGZ wants to protect against violence and provide assistance.

128162.explanation: Within Zwartewaterland, there are already possibilities to build extra homes on farms in the rural area. BGZ considers this acceptable.

128163.explanation: Where possible, society may demand something back from benefit recipients.

128167.explanation: At this moment, the municipality has sufficient coverage and where necessary, these can be applied for.

128169.explanation: BGZ is a strong supporter of freedom of choice.

128172.explanation: Dog tax is okay, provided that facilities and maintenance are provided for it.

128175.explanation: Each village must be able to carefully weigh this itself, and where there is a need and it fits in the village, it must be allowed.

128179.explanation: If there is need and demand, this application must be considered.

128181.explanation: We must use space sparingly and it must be appropriate for our municipality. BGZ does not support taller residential buildings than those already realized in our municipality.

128182.explanation: Renting out individual rooms should be allowed. Although we don't have many student houses in Zwartewaterland, we do have young people who want to take a step towards adulthood.

128187.explanation: Currently, every locality has a youth center. BGZ wants to maintain this, with a role for youth workers included.

128131.explanation: Quality is more important than a male/female quota. However, for a good team, it is desirable to have both women and men in the group.

128132.explanation: Every 4 years, the residents of Zwartewaterland can make their voices heard during the municipal elections. In addition, there should be as much participation as possible before a decision is made. Good and careful consultation before a decision is our path to good democracy.

128136.explanation: To collectively achieve climate goals, the municipality should play a supporting role, including financially, for example, to pay for research or start-up costs. This way we can achieve the goals more easily.

128137.explanation: Space is scarce. New developments in industrial areas almost always come at the expense of green spaces or farmland. Distribution centers are known to occupy a lot of space while employing few people.

128138.explanation: A large-scale asylum seeker center does not fit with Zwartewaterland. However, we must fulfill our obligation to accommodate status holders. This does not have to be in an asylum center, but can also be in tiny houses or temporary housing.

128143.explanation: If the fireworks ban is implemented, it is better for streets or neighborhoods to set off fireworks themselves. This should be allowed in an organized association context. It is good for social cohesion. The municipality should make it easy for fireworks to be set off, but should not organize its own show.

128144.explanation: The polluter pays: This is truly the responsibility of the residents themselves. However, it must be easy to hand in waste.

128147.explanation: Yes, and not just for themselves, but when a farmyard becomes available, a few houses can be built for sale. It must fit the landscape, so it should be small-scale.

128149.explanation: Local media ensure that residents are well informed about what local politics and the municipal government are doing. This is valuable for residents to see what is happening clearly and reliably.

128151.explanation: The municipality should encourage schools and music/cultural providers to collaborate.

128152.explanation: The municipality should mainly be supportive. It is important that associations continue to exist. The municipality can contribute financially, for example by helping associations become financially healthy again by connecting them with volunteers. But not just handing out money without conditions.

128154.explanation: Every locality has its own library, and that should remain so.

128155.explanation: All rules in Zwartewaterland are democratically established. If we make rules, we must all adhere to them, otherwise issues like parking nuisance or arbitrariness arise. It's not pleasant to issue fines, but if it leads to better coexistence, it is necessary.

128156.explanation: Camera surveillance is sometimes appropriate in industrial areas. In residential neighborhoods, we find this inappropriate. As CDA, we do not want to monitor our residents.

128157.explanation: Zwartewaterland is too small for such a facility and this is well organized regionally.

128162.explanation: Housing shortage is high, so add a neighborhood or street. Also at the edges of the city. At the same time, possibilities in the centers should be considered, as for example elderly people prefer to live close to amenities.

128163.explanation: Agreed, but according to ability. This may mean asking for volunteer work. Only if you cannot do this due to illness, there should be room for that.

128167.explanation: More electric charging points are needed, but at a pace that matches usage. CDA also supports that people without their own driveway can charge near their home.

128169.explanation: Residents should have the choice of which care provider to visit, if this is important for their personal beliefs. The municipality should, however, negotiate the same rates with different providers.

128172.explanation: As long as the revenue is used for waste bins and maintenance of walking paths, the dog tax is fine.

128179.explanation: A coffee shop does not fit in Zwartewaterland. If you promote a healthy lifestyle, you should not also allow coffee shops.

128181.explanation: To build more homes per square meter, it is good to build higher. In our beautiful landscape, however, we should limit this to 4 to 5 residential floors.

128182.explanation: The municipality should not prohibit it, but also not allow it to grow indefinitely. The latter is not realistic in Zwartewaterland. But there should be no uncontrolled growth of, for example, migrant worker housing. Good rules have now been made for this.

128187.explanation: Yes, meeting places are important for young people. Every locality should have one. This can work well. It is a good place where youth workers can get in touch with youth.

128131.explanation: For a good reflection of the community, it is good to have as equal a balance as possible between men and women.

128132.explanation: Participation is very important; for this we have, among other things, a citizen panel. Although there is always room for improvement, it functions satisfactorily.

128136.explanation: We must move away from fossil energy. Energy cooperatives therefore deserve support from the municipality.

128137.explanation: Business parks in Zwartewaterland can hardly expand. The scarce space is mainly for the needs of local businesses. Space-consuming projects, such as distribution centers that barely bring high-quality employment, are not wanted here.

128138.explanation: As Zwartewaterland, we must also contribute to the reception of refugees. The distribution law divides this task among all municipalities. It is therefore only fair that Zwartewaterland also provides its share.

128143.explanation: When a total fireworks ban comes into effect, it is good that the municipality, to encourage the collective celebration of New Year's Eve, organizes or has an activity organized in the localities. This does not have to be a fireworks show, but could equally be a music festival.

128144.explanation: We must handle our waste carefully. So first think well about how to reuse items, prevent or reduce waste. Free pickup sends the wrong signal. Moreover... waste processing is not free!

128147.explanation: This would only lead to a huge cluttering of the rural area. If a farmer stops, it should be possible to build a limited number of houses on the farmyard where barns are demolished, which fit the landscape.

128149.explanation: That depends on the role a local broadcaster plays within the community. When it fulfills a clear informative function, that is fine (think of the Corona period), but if only music is broadcast, that is not a task for the municipality. There are plenty of music stations.

128151.explanation: For many children, school will be their first introduction to music and playing an instrument this way. We want to encourage that. Subsidies should depend on the parents' income.

128152.explanation: That depends on the cause of the difficulties and whether there is a prospect of a healthy, independent continuation. Associations already qualify for subsidies, but in exceptional cases an additional appeal to the municipality should be possible.

128154.explanation: The library is a basic facility. Information must be available close to the citizen. Moreover, libraries increasingly serve as meeting places and help desks.

128155.explanation: Unfortunately, we do not automatically follow the rules imposed by the government. Supervision and enforcement, including fines at times, are therefore necessary. If we don't follow the rules, unsafe situations (traffic) or illegal situations (constructions, housing) arise.

128156.explanation: Zwartewaterland does not need that; the scale of our communities and the solidarity provide enough mutual involvement to make camera surveillance unnecessary. Moreover, it infringes on our privacy. In some places, such as bicycle parking facilities, cameras can be helpful.

128157.explanation: Of course, domestic violence also occurs in our municipality. A women's shelter must also ensure that (mostly) women are not easily confronted by the perpetrator of the violence. The scale of Zwartewaterland makes that impossible.

128162.explanation: That is not always possible; where it can, we will of course first fill existing open spaces within the cores, but the growth of Zwartewaterland also makes expansion of our cores necessary.

128163.explanation: But only according to ability... Participating in the labor process, in any way, gives meaning to your existence and can also be a first step towards a 'regular' job. But we also accept that there are people for whom this is not possible.

128167.explanation: This development cannot be stopped, so new places must be added. But preferably in a smart way so that as many electric car owners as possible can use the same locations. Because parking is already a problem especially in the cores.

128169.explanation: In principle yes, but there are also financial limits. Care for everyone who needs it can only be provided if total care also remains affordable. Too much fragmentation of care offerings often means too high costs per individual client.

128172.explanation: Dog tax is 'something from the past'. No tax is paid on any pet, so why should dogs be an exception? Moreover, it is a 'dog's job' to precisely determine who owns one or more dogs and who does not.

128175.explanation: Apart from religious considerations, which we must take into account in our municipality, it is good for every person to have at least one day of rest per week.

128179.explanation: Zwartewaterland pursues a policy focused on prevention and a healthy life. Narcotic substances do not fit within that. Enabling a coffee shop is therefore undesirable.

128181.explanation: Really tall residential buildings do not fit a rural municipality like Zwartewaterland. However, stacked construction up to 4-5 floors should also be possible in our cores, also because our rural area is unsuitable for expansion. So, no wasteful use of space.

128182.explanation: Renting out individual rooms / hospitahousing can be a solution for young people who are still looking for affordable own housing. So we are against an absolute ban. However, it must be prevented that this leads to exploitation by unscrupulous landlords.

128187.explanation: Young people need their own meeting place. A youth center can fulfill that function. It is important to realize, however, that not all young people are the same. Groups have different needs and a single center will not meet everyone's needs.

128132.explanation: Where it can be assumed that the council creates the most realistic possible picture of all aspects of the decision

128136.explanation: The start to achieve joint generation may receive financial support, but further development lies with the initiators

128138.explanation: Our municipality has always taken the lead in housing people in difficult situations (Ukrainians); it is now time for others to also contribute their part

128143.explanation: Only when there is a total fireworks ban and that can also be enforced

128144.explanation: Too much bulky household waste still disappears in rural areas or in places where it does not belong

128147.explanation: No, the zoning plan or environmental vision is leading here

128149.explanation: Certainly the local broadcaster because it can directly inform citizens, but regional facility support may also be obtained through the local broadcaster

128151.explanation: Musical education is a basic need

128152.explanation: However, possible financial support can be sought for attracting funds and maintenance of fields or buildings

128154.explanation: If the larger library is accessible to everyone as well

128155.explanation: We now have to hire enforcement officers from larger municipalities, so there is a need for this

128156.explanation: Especially on the industrial sites, camera surveillance should not be missing, and camera surveillance as a means of prevention is an option for the various centers

128157.explanation: The municipality is too small for that and good facilities are nearby

128163.explanation: In several countries this has long been common practice and people remain in the labor market

128167.explanation: It must be in line with the number of fossil fuel users and electric vehicles

128169.explanation: Here too, quality is the starting point

128172.explanation: Agreed if no facilities are given back to the dog owner and it is only used as a last resort

128175.explanation: There are no shopping Sundays yet, but if there is demand from the SMEs, that should not be a barrier. A trial during the construction holiday could be started

128179.explanation: There is a large illegal circuit, so regulation would be appropriate to reduce this

128181.explanation: If this would contribute to solving the housing shortage, but then only in locations where it is aesthetically responsible

128182.explanation: That is up to the owner of the properties

128187.explanation: In two cores there is already a youth center and one should also be established in Hasselt. It is a meeting place for young people

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Zwartewaterland.
Residents of Zwartewaterland should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zwartewaterland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Zwartewaterland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Zwartewaterland should install more CCTV/camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Zwartewaterland should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
There should be one larger library in Zwartewaterland instead of smaller libraries in every village.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Zwartewaterland should open a youth center in every population center.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
